title: Request a decision on lease price and terms of acquisition: Form Leasehold 9
body: "There are 6 types of applications you can make with this form.  You can make a collective enfranchisement of flats application under:    section 24(1) of the Leasehold Reform, Housing and Urban Development Act 1993 – if the landlord is known  section 27 of the Leasehold Reform, Housing and Urban Development Act 1993 – if the landlord is missing   You can make a lease renewal of flats application under:    section 48(1) of the Leasehold Reform, Housing and Urban Development Act 1993 – if the landlord is known  section 51 (1) to (5) of the Leasehold Reform, Housing and Urban Development Act 1993 – if the landlord is missing   You can make an enfranchisement of a house application under:    section 21 of the Leasehold Reform Act 1967 – if the landlord is known  section 21(1)(ba) and 21(2) of the Leasehold Reform Act 1967 – if the landlord is missing   Read guidance on leasehold enfranchisement and what happens at the First-tier Tribunal.  Residential leaseholders can get free government funded, independent advice from LEASE, the Leasehold Advisory Service.  Open a document  You can download and open PDF documents on your device. Downloading enables more features to be used, such as printing.  Adobe Reader is a free to use PDF viewer. You can use this to view, fill in and print PDF documents. You do not need to sign up for a free trial.  Follow these steps:    Download Adobe Reader for free. Right-click on the document link then select ‘Save link as’ or ‘Download linked file’. Save the form (in your ‘documents’ folder, for example). Open Adobe Reader and then select the saved form.   If the form still does not open, contact hmctsforms@justice.gov.uk.  If you need a printed version contact your local court.  Find more court and tribunal forms by category.  Find out how HM Courts & Tribunals Service uses personal information you give when you fill in a form. "
